EnglishDescription: Abu Sulemaan bin Urma says: I went to Samarra at the time of Mutawakkil’s reign and met Saeed-e-Hajib (the gatekeeper) whom Mutawakkil had handed over Imam Naqi (a) so that he murders his holiness. When I reached him, he asked: do you wish to witness your Lord? I replied: Glorious and Mighty is my Lord, no vision can grasp Him and naked eyes are incapable of witnessing Him. He said: The same man whom you believe that he is your Imam. I said: I would not hesitate to witness him. He said: I have ordered for his assassination and will assassinate him tomorrow; there is a messenger near him right now, you can meet him when he departs. After a short while, that messenger departed from Imam and Saeed e Hajib asked me to go near Imam (a). I entered the house in which Imam was imprisoned. I saw his holiness standing near a grave that was already dug; I went inside and greeted him, then wept severely. His holiness asked: Why are you weeping? I said: Because of what I witness. He replied: Do not weep because of it, this act will not be committed by them. I felt relaxed on hearing his reply. Then his holiness said: He will not live for more than two days until Allah sheds his blood and the blood of his friend whom you met. Sulemaan says: By God, not two days had passed that he was killed. I said to Imam Naqi (a): This Holy Prophet (a) once said: Do not envy the days so they envy you in return. His holiness said: Indeed, although this narration has an interpretation: Saturday is for the Holy Prophet (s). Sunday is for the commander of the faithful (a). Monday is for Imam Hasan and Imam Husain (a). Tuesday is for Ali bin Husain, Mohammad bin Ali and Jafar bin Mohammad (a). Wednesday is for Musa bin Jafar, Ali bin Musa, Mohammad bin Ali and for me (Ali bin Mohammad) (a). Thursday is for my son, Hasan (a). Friday is for the Riser of the Holy Progeny (a).
RUrduDescription: Abu Sulemaan bin Urma kehte hai; Mutawakkil-e-Abbaasi ke zamaane mein (shaher) Saamrrah gaya ta-keh Saeed Haajib (watchman) se mulaqaat karu. Mutawakkil ne Imam Haadi (a) ke qatl ka hukm usey diya tha, jab mai uske paas gaya to us ne meri taraf rukh kar ke kaha; kya tum apne Khuda ko dekhna chahte ho? Ham ne kaha; Subhaanallah mera Khuda aankh se dekha nahi ja sakta. Us ne kaha; meri muraad woh shakhs hai jise tum Imam samajhte ho. Ham ne kaha; yeh hamein bura na lagega. Kehne laga; mujh ko unke qatl ka hukm diya gaya hai aur mai kal yeh kaam anjaam dunga, is waqt ek shakhs ko unke paas bheja hai jab woh baaher aaye to tum unke paas jaawo, thodi der ke baad woh waapas aaya aur kehne laga tum unke paas ja sakte ho. Mai us ghar mein daakhil huwa jis mein Hazrat (a) ko qaid kiya tha, dekha Hazrat baiythay hai aur unke muqaabil ek qabr khodi gayi hai, Hazrat ko salaam kiya aur jo mazaalim Hazrat ke upar dhaae (kiye) gaye thay un par shiddad se girya kiya. Hazrat ne farmaya; kyu’n girya kar rahe ho? Ham ne kaha; us sitam ke liye jo aap par karna chahte hai. Farmaya; Is amr ke liye girya mat karo chun-keh woh hamein nuqsaan nahi pohoncha sakte, phir farmaya; do (2) roz bhi na guzrega keh Parwardigaar-e-Aalam Mutawakkil ke Haajib ko halaak kar dega. Raawi ka bayaan hai keh abhi do (2) roz bhi na guzra tha keh woh qatl ho gaya. Imam Haadi (a) se arz kiya; Rasool-e-Khuda (s) se ek hadees waarid huwi hai lekin mai uska maanee (matlab) nahi samajh pa raha hu, aap samjah de. Rasool-e-Khuda (s) ne farmaya; “Waqt ke saath dushmani na karo is liye keh woh bhi tum se dushmani karega.” Hazrat (a) ne farmaya; haa’n Rasool-e-Khuda (s) ki hadees ki taaweel honi chahiye, ayaam se muraad ham Ahlebait-e-Risaalat (a) hai. Sambeh (Saturday) Rasool-e-Khuda (s) ka din hai. Yek-Shambeh (Sunday) Ameerul Momeneen (a) se makhsoos hai. Do-Shambeh (Monday) Imam Hasan (a) wa Imam Husain (a) ka din hai. Se-Shambah (Tuesday): Imam Zainul Aabedin (a), Imam Muhammad Baqir (a) wa Imam Jafar Sadiq (a) se makhsoos hai. Chaar-Shambeh (Wednesday): Imam Musa Kazim (a), Imam Ali Reza (a), Imam-e-Jawaad (a) aur Mera din hai. Panch-Shambeh (Thursday): Mere farzand Imam Hasan Askari (a) ka din hai. Aur Juma (Friday) ham Ahlebait ke Qaaem (a) se makhsoos hai.
